technology.title: ‘It does feel like an intimidation campaign’: why is US tech giant Palantir suing a small Swiss magazine?
technology.summary: An investigation by journalists working with Republik magazine may have struck a nerve by suggesting the company has failed in Switzerland It was over beers on an autumn evening in Zurich in 2024 that a group of journalists with an independent Swiss research collective began to discuss investigating Palantir, one of the world’s biggest tech companies. Three years earlier, Palantir had advertised that it was setting up a “European hub” in the Swiss municipality of Altendorf, a sleepy town of roughly 7,000 people on the shores of Lake Zurich. Continue reading...

briefing.news_focus: An investigation by journalists working with Republik magazine may have struck a nerve by suggesting the company has failed in Switzerland It was over beers on an autumn evening in Zurich in 2024 that a group of journalists with an independent Swiss research collective began to discuss investigating Palantir, one of the world’s biggest tech companies. Three years earlier, Palantir had advertised that it was setting up a “European hub” in the Swiss municipality of Altendorf, a sleepy town of roughly 7,000 people on the shores of Lake Zurich. Continue reading...
briefing.news_focus_zh: 美國科技巨頭Palantir對瑞士小型雜誌Republik提起訴訟，原因是該雜誌的調查報導質疑Palantir在瑞士的業務表現，顯示出該公司對於負面報導的敏感性。
briefing.background: Palantir是一家專注於數據分析的科技公司，近年來在全球擴展其業務，並在瑞士設立了歐洲樞紐。該公司在瑞士的投資引發了媒體的關注，特別是在其業務表現未達預期的情況下。Republik雜誌的調查報導可能揭示了Palantir在當地市場的挑戰，並引發了該公司對媒體自由的反應，顯示出科技公司在面對負面報導時的脆弱性。
briefing.analysis: Palantir的訴訟行為可能被視為對媒體自由的威脅，並引發了對企業如何處理負面報導的討論。對於Palantir而言，維護品牌形象和市場信任至關重要，而對於Republik雜誌及其支持者來說，這一事件則凸顯了獨立媒體在監督企業行為中的重要性。此事件可能促使其他科技公司重新評估與媒體的關係，並引發對言論自由的更廣泛討論。

technology.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
technology.summary: Using OpenClaw with Claude AI is about to get a lot more expensive, thanks to Anthropic's new policy changes. Beginning April 4th at 3PM ET, users will "no longer be able to use your Claude subscription limits for third-party harnesses including OpenClaw," according to an email sent to users on Friday evening. Instead, if users […]

briefing.news_focus: Using OpenClaw with Claude AI is about to get a lot more expensive, thanks to Anthropic's new policy changes. Beginning April 4th at 3PM ET, users will "no longer be able to use your Claude subscription limits for third-party harnesses including OpenClaw," according to an email sent to users on Friday evening. Instead, if users […]
briefing.news_focus_zh: Anthropic 宣布自 4 月 4 日起，使用 OpenClaw 與 Claude AI 的訂閱者需額外支付費用，無法再使用原有的訂閱限制，這一政策改變將顯著提高使用成本。
briefing.background: Anthropic 是一家專注於人工智慧的公司，Claude 是其推出的 AI 模型。OpenClaw 作為第三方應用，允許用戶更靈活地使用 Claude 的功能。隨著 AI 市場的競爭加劇，企業為了保護自身利益，逐漸對第三方應用施加限制，這一政策變化反映了市場對於平台控制權的重視。
briefing.analysis: 此政策改變可能會引發用戶的不滿，因為額外的費用將影響使用者的選擇和使用體驗。對於 Anthropic 而言，這是保護其商業模式和獲利的策略，但也可能導致用戶轉向其他 AI 服務。競爭對手如 OpenAI 和 Google 可能會利用這一機會吸引受影響的用戶，進一步加劇市場競爭。

supporting_titles: Anthropic Says That Claude Contains Its Own Kind of Emotions

technology.title: Meta Pauses Work With Mercor After Data Breach Puts AI Industry Secrets at Risk
technology.summary: Major AI labs are investigating a security incident that impacted Mercor, a leading data vendor. The incident could have exposed key data about how they train AI models.

briefing.news_focus: Major AI labs are investigating a security incident that impacted Mercor, a leading data vendor. The incident could have exposed key data about how they train AI models.
briefing.news_focus_zh: Meta 暫停與數據供應商 Mercor 的合作，因為一宗安全事件可能洩露了關鍵的 AI 模型訓練數據，這引起了主要 AI 實驗室的關注。
briefing.background: 隨著 AI 技術的迅速發展，數據安全成為業界的重要議題。Mercor 作為一家領先的數據供應商，為多家 AI 實驗室提供訓練數據。此次安全事件可能影響到 AI 模型的訓練方式，進一步引發對數據保護和商業機密的擔憂。這一事件發生在 AI 競爭日益激烈的背景下，企業對數據的依賴程度加深，使得數據洩露的風險也隨之上升。
briefing.analysis: 此次事件對 Meta 及其他 AI 實驗室的影響深遠。Meta 暫停與 Mercor 的合作，顯示出對數據安全的高度重視，可能會導致其 AI 研發進度受阻。其他 AI 實驗室也可能重新評估與 Mercor 的合作關係，進一步影響該公司的市場地位。對於 Mercor 來說，這次事件可能損害其聲譽，並引發客戶對其數據安全措施的質疑，未來需要加強透明度和安全防護。

supporting_titles: Europe’s cyber agency blames hacking gangs for massive data breach and leak
‘Thank God they’re still alive’: Kaiser therapists claim its new screening system puts patients at higher risk by delaying their care

politics.title: Ukraine war briefing: Russian army records almost no territorial gains for first time since 2023, analysis shows
politics.summary: Russian advances slowing, thinktank’s data shows; 14 killed in Ukraine in massive drone and missile salvo. What we know on day 1,501 Russia’s army recorded almost no territorial gains on the frontline in Ukraine in March for the first time in two-and-a-half years , according to analysis of data from the Institute for the Study of War (ISW) conducted by Agence France-Presse. The Russian army has been slowing in its advances since late 2025 – because of Kyiv’s localised breakthroughs in the south-east of the country. Across the entire frontline, the Russian army seized only 23 sq km (8.9 sq miles) in March, losing territory in some areas, according to the analysis. This figure excludes infiltration operations conducted by Russian forces beyond the frontline, as well as advances claimed by the Russian side but neither confirmed nor denied by the ISW. The Russian army made 319 sq km of gains in January and 123 sq km in February, which was then the smallest advance since April 2024. Its advance in March was the smallest since September 2023. The ISW attributed the slowdown to Ukrainian counteroffensives, but also to “Russia’s ban on using Starlink terminals in Ukraine” and “the Kremlin’s efforts to restrict access to Telegram” . The messaging app – very popular among Russians, including those fighting on the front – has been barely usable in recent months due to blocks imposed by the authorities . As in February, Russia lost ground on the southern section of the frontline, between the Donetsk and Dnipropetrovsk regions. Russian strikes killed 14 people in Ukraine on Friday, officials said, as Moscow launched the latest in an increasing number of daytime barrages . Moscow has been firing aerial broadsides at Ukraine throughout its more than four-year invasion, mostly at night, but in recent weeks has stepped up daytime attacks. The Russian military used more than 500 drones and dozens of missiles in its salvo on Friday, according to the Ukrainian air force. Russia’s Baltic oil export hubs at Ust-Luga and Primorsk remain unable to handle shipments after a series of Ukrainian drone attacks, prompting the country’s refineries to find alternative routes for export , industry sources said on Friday. The attacks have damaged port infrastructure and continued through the last two weeks of March, with at least five strikes on Ust-Luga in the space of 10 days. Sources said the export restrictions, along with disruptions at large refineries, could lead to a decrease in oil production in Russia. Traders said refineries had been unable to deliver diesel fuel to Primorsk for export since 22 March, leaving refineries in European Russia and Siberia without their most viable export route. Traders said refineries were having to consider more expensive rail transport routes to other export terminals. A Ukrainian drone and missile attack on southern Russia killed at least one person, injured four others and sparked a blaze aboard a foreign-flagged vessel , Russian officials said on Saturday. Zelenskyy has called on lawmakers to pass key legislation next week to avert a funding crisis , help Ukraine fight the war against Russia, and enact key reforms required for EU accession. Due to lagging reforms and slow legislative progress in late 2025 and early this year, Ukraine missed deadlines to unlock billions from its key lenders, economists said. With the need for external financing standing at $52bn this year – equivalent to about a quarter of annual economic output – the budget situation is desperate. “I have a list of key draft laws that are critical for securing funding,” the Ukrainian president said in remarks released on Friday. They range from strengthening the court system to reforming energy sector procedures. “I believe that members of parliament from all parties must understand the importance of these bills for Ukraine’s budget,” said Zelenskyy, who has a majority in parliament but its relations with his government have soured. Continue reading...

This figure excludes infiltration operations conducted by Russian forces beyond the frontline, as well as advances claimed by the Russian side but neither confirmed nor denied by the ISW. The Russian army made 319 sq km of gains in January and 123 sq km in February, which was then the smallest advance since April 2024. Its advance in March was the smallest since September 2023. The ISW attributed the slowdown to Ukrainian counteroffensives, but also to “Russia’s ban on using Starlink terminals in Ukraine” and “the Kremlin’s efforts to restrict access to Telegram” . The attacks have damaged port infrastructure and continued through the last two weeks of March, with at least five strikes on Ust-Luga in the space of 10 days. With the need for external financing standing at $52bn this year – equivalent to about a quarter of annual economic output – the budget situation is desperate. “I have a list of key draft laws that are critical for securing funding,” the Ukrainian president said in remarks released on Friday. They range from strengthening the court system to reforming energy sector procedures. “I believe that members of parliament from all parties must understand the importance of these bills for Ukraine’s budget,” said Zelenskyy, who has a majority in parliament but its relations with his government have soured. Continue reading...
briefing.news_focus_zh: 根據分析，俄軍在烏克蘭的前線自2023年以來首次未有實質領土增長，顯示出其進攻速度減緩。烏克蘭的反攻以及俄方對通訊工具的限制被認為是主要原因。俄方在最近的攻擊中造成14人死亡，並面臨石油出口困難。
briefing.background: 自2022年俄烏戰爭爆發以來，俄軍在前線的進展一直是國際關注的焦點。2023年以來，俄軍的領土增長逐漸減少，特別是在烏克蘭的反攻行動下。烏克蘭政府在面對外部資金需求的同時，也在推動必要的內部改革，以促進歐盟的入盟進程。此時，俄方的通訊限制措施也影響了其軍事行動的效率。
briefing.analysis: 俄軍的進展減緩對於克里姆林宮而言是一個重大挫折，可能導致內部壓力增加，尤其是在面對經濟困境和軍事失利的情況下。烏克蘭則在反攻中獲得了戰略優勢，並且急需外部資金以維持戰爭努力。這使得烏克蘭政府必須加快立法進程，以確保國際支持。各方在此局勢中，俄方面臨的軍事與經濟挑戰將影響其未來的戰略選擇。

supporting_titles: Russia made nearly no territorial gains in Ukraine in March: Analysis
Video shows the moment a Russian drone hit an apartment in Ukraine
8 die in Ukraine in Russian strikes as Kyiv holds door open for Easter truce
Iran War Opens New Markets for Kazakhstan

politics.title: Competition or ‘co-opetition’: how is convergence shaping AI race between China and US?
politics.summary: There was a surprise guest speaker at Nvidia’s widely watched GPU Technology Conference (GTC) in San Jose, California, last month: Yang Zhilin, the founder of Beijing-based Moonshot AI, the developer behind the Kimi family of foundational artificial intelligence models. Amid heated rhetoric about US-China AI competition, which some have likened to an “arms race”, the participation of a Chinese AI start-up’s CEO at the flagship event of American chipmaking giant Nvidia might have struck some as...

briefing.news_focus: There was a surprise guest speaker at Nvidia’s widely watched GPU Technology Conference (GTC) in San Jose, California, last month: Yang Zhilin, the founder of Beijing-based Moonshot AI, the developer behind the Kimi family of foundational artificial intelligence models. Amid heated rhetoric about US-China AI competition, which some have likened to an “arms race”, the participation of a Chinese AI start-up’s CEO at the flagship event of American chipmaking giant Nvidia might have struck some as...
briefing.news_focus_zh: 在美國加州舉行的Nvidia GPU技術大會上，中國初創公司Moonshot AI的創始人楊志林意外出席，這在美中AI競爭的背景下引發關注。此事件突顯了在激烈的科技競爭中，合作與競爭並存的複雜局面。
briefing.background: 隨著人工智慧技術的迅速發展，美國與中國之間的競爭日益加劇，許多專家將其比作軍備競賽。美國在半導體和AI技術領域的領先地位面臨來自中國的挑戰，後者在政策支持下迅速崛起。Nvidia作為全球領先的GPU製造商，其技術在AI發展中扮演關鍵角色，吸引了全球AI企業的關注。
briefing.analysis: 楊志林的出席可能象徵著中美兩國在AI領域的合作潛力，儘管競爭激烈，但也顯示出雙方在技術交流上的可能性。美方可能擔心中國企業的崛起會威脅其技術優勢，而中國則希望借助美國的技術平台加速自身發展。這種「合作競爭」的模式可能會影響未來的政策走向及市場動態，促使雙方在某些領域尋求共贏。

supporting_titles: Middle East crisis live: US and Iran race to recover missing pilot from downed jet; another ship passes through strait of Hormuz
China says peace talks between Afghanistan and Pakistan are advancing
China and African Sovereignty: Beyond the Digital Silk Road Narrative

politics.title: Voters in Wales failed by inaccurate UK media reports on devolved issues, study finds
politics.summary: Reports on English policies seen in Wales as relating to whole of UK contribute to widespread confusion, researchers say UK media is failing to report properly on devolved issues in Wales, leaving voters ill-informed about May’s Senedd elections, a report has found. A Cardiff University study of more than 3,000 news items found repeated patterns in coverage across different broadcasters and platforms, including not signposting whether an issue was relevant to England or England and Wales only, widespread references to “the government” rather than “the UK government”, and the use of “you” and “your” in contexts that apply only to people living in England. Continue reading...

briefing.news_focus: Reports on English policies seen in Wales as relating to whole of UK contribute to widespread confusion, researchers say UK media is failing to report properly on devolved issues in Wales, leaving voters ill-informed about May’s Senedd elections, a report has found. A Cardiff University study of more than 3,000 news items found repeated patterns in coverage across different broadcasters and platforms, including not signposting whether an issue was relevant to England or England and Wales only, widespread references to “the government” rather than “the UK government”, and the use of “you” and “your” in contexts that apply only to people living in England. Continue reading...
briefing.news_focus_zh: 一項研究指出，英國媒體在報導威爾斯的權力下放議題時存在不準確性，導致選民對即將舉行的威爾斯議會選舉感到困惑。研究發現，媒體未能清楚區分英國與威爾斯的政策，影響了選民的知識與參與度。
briefing.background: 威爾斯自1999年以來擁有自己的議會，負責地方政策的制定。然而，英國主流媒體在報導時常常將英國政策與威爾斯的權力下放問題混淆，未能清楚標示哪些政策僅適用於英格蘭，這導致選民對地方政治的理解不足。隨著2021年威爾斯議會選舉的臨近，這一問題愈發凸顯，影響了民主參與的質量。
briefing.analysis: 此報告揭示了英國媒體在報導地方議題時的系統性缺陷，對威爾斯選民的影響深遠。對於威爾斯的選民來說，缺乏準確資訊可能導致他們在選舉中做出不知情的選擇，進一步削弱地方政府的合法性。英國政府與威爾斯議會之間的緊張關係也可能因媒體報導的不當而加劇，影響政策的推動與執行。媒體的角色在於提供準確的資訊，若無法做到，將可能引發更大的政治與社會問題。

supporting_titles: Pakistan rejects media reports of collapse of initiative to facilitate U.S., Iran talks

economy.title: Americans can still get a 2% mortgage
economy.summary: At a time of high interest rates, there are bargains to be found

briefing.news_focus: At a time of high interest rates, there are bargains to be found
briefing.news_focus_zh: 在高利率的環境下，美國市場仍然存在可獲得2%利率的抵押貸款選擇，顯示出市場中的一些利率優惠機會。
briefing.background: 隨著美國聯邦儲備系統為了控制通脹而提高利率，抵押貸款利率普遍上升，導致購房成本增加。然而，部分貸款機構仍提供較低利率的抵押貸款，這可能是由於競爭激烈或特定市場需求所驅動。這一現象在過去幾年中，尤其是在疫情後的經濟復甦階段，顯得尤為突出。
briefing.analysis: 對於潛在的房屋買家來說，能夠獲得2%的抵押貸款無疑是一個吸引人的機會，尤其是在高利率的背景下。然而，這也可能意味著貸款機構在風險管理上採取了不同的策略，可能會對借款人的信用評分或其他條件提出更高的要求。此外，這種情況可能會吸引投資者進一步進入房地產市場，推高房價，並可能對整體經濟產生影響。

economy.title: One year on from Trump tariffs, Taiwan sees 80% rise in exports to US

briefing.news_focus: One year on from Trump tariffs, Taiwan sees 80% rise in exports to US
briefing.news_focus_zh: 台灣在特朗普關稅政策實施一年後，對美國的出口增長了80%。
briefing.background: 特朗普政府於2018年對中國商品徵收高額關稅，這使得許多企業尋求將供應鏈轉移至其他國家以避免關稅影響。台灣作為亞洲重要的電子產品生產基地，受益於這一政策，吸引了大量美國企業將生產轉移至台灣，進而促進了台灣對美國的出口增長。
briefing.analysis: 台灣的出口增長反映了美國企業對於供應鏈多元化的需求，尤其是在中美貿易緊張局勢下。這對台灣經濟而言是一大利好，促進了產業升級與投資。然而，隨著拜登政府的上台，貿易政策可能會有所調整，台灣需警惕未來可能的政策變化對出口的影響。此外，中國可能會加強對台灣的經濟壓力，進一步影響兩岸關係。

supporting_titles: ‘If he’d stayed on the golf course, we’d be in a better place’: experts on Trump’s tariffs, one year on
Who will win from Trump’s tariffs?
A year on: Four ways Trump's tariffs have changed the global economy

economy.title: Cost of living: get ready for ‘awful April’ bill increases
economy.summary: From council tax to water, broadband to stamps, the annual round of price rises starts on 1 April … and that’s before any fallout from Iran war UK savers told to act now before Easter Sunday cash Isa deadline Britons will typically see more than £200 added to their household bills this year as “awful April” price increases kick in. The annual rises are particularly unwelcome as the financial turmoil caused by the Middle East conflict has pushed up mortgage rates, fuel prices and energy bills for rural households . Continue reading...

briefing.news_focus: From council tax to water, broadband to stamps, the annual round of price rises starts on 1 April … and that’s before any fallout from Iran war UK savers told to act now before Easter Sunday cash Isa deadline Britons will typically see more than £200 added to their household bills this year as “awful April” price increases kick in. The annual rises are particularly unwelcome as the financial turmoil caused by the Middle East conflict has pushed up mortgage rates, fuel prices and energy bills for rural households . Continue reading...
briefing.news_focus_zh: 英國即將迎來4月的價格上漲，家庭開支將增加超過200英鎊，這一漲幅受到中東衝突影響，導致抵押貸款利率、燃料價格及能源費用上升。
briefing.background: 隨著4月的來臨，英國各項生活成本將迎來年度調整，包括市政稅、水費、寬頻及郵票等，這些漲幅在當前經濟不穩定的背景下尤為嚴重。中東地區的衝突導致全球能源市場波動，進一步推高了英國的生活成本，特別是對於居住在偏遠地區的家庭影響更大。
briefing.analysis: 此次價格上漲對英國家庭的財務狀況造成直接衝擊，尤其是在經濟已經因中東衝突而承受壓力的情況下。政府可能面臨民生問題的壓力，需考慮如何平衡經濟穩定與民眾需求。銀行和金融機構則需應對因利率上升而帶來的貸款風險，可能會影響貸款發放政策。此外，消費者的購買力下降可能會對零售業造成進一步影響，形成惡性循環。

theme_of_day: 今日新聞整體呈現出科技、政治與經濟三大領域的相互交織與影響。在科技版面中，Palantir的訴訟與Meta的數據洩漏事件反映出科技巨頭在面對外部挑戰時的脆弱性，這與政治版面中烏克蘭戰爭的進展及美中AI競爭的複雜性形成鮮明對比。此外，經濟版面報導的台灣對美出口增長與英國生活成本上升，則顯示了全球市場動態如何受到地緣政治與科技發展的影響，進一步強化了各版面之間的聯繫。
